title: Steve Kerr explains how the Celtics went from an ‘afterthought’ to a ‘real favorite’
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Steve Kerr praises Celtics' transformation, calling them a 'favorite' after overcoming early-season challenges.
Celtics thrived without Jayson Tatum, developing young players and winning games, now boosted by Tatum's return.
Boston Celtics rank second in offensive efficiency, excelling at spacing and teamwork under coach Joe Mazzulla.

title: Advocates sue Worcester over response to mental health 911 calls, alleging discrimination
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Advocates sued Worcester, claiming the city's 911 policy discriminates by sending armed police to mental health calls.
The lawsuit argues there's no equivalent emergency response for mental health crises compared to physical health emergencies.
Fewer than 25% of Worcester police have completed crisis intervention training, per the Department of Mental Health.

title: Hilary Duff will be Northeastern University’s 2026 commencement speaker
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Hilary Duff will be the 2026 commencement speaker for Northeastern University, announced in a Y2K-themed social media post.
Duff, known for 'Lizzy Maguire' and 'A Cinderella Story,' will speak at Fenway Park on April 29, 2026.
Past Northeastern commencement speakers include David A. Thomas, David Muir, and Coretta Scott King, among others.

title: 3 roster needs Patriots must still address after free-agency spending spree
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Patriots seek a proven No. 1 wide receiver, with A.J. Brown as a potential trade target, to enhance their offense.
New England looks to strengthen their pass rush, targeting edge-rushers like Texas A&M's Cashius Howell in the draft.
Patriots aim to add depth at offensive tackle, considering prospects like Clemson's Blake Miller for potential long-term development.

title: Needham 16-year-old charged with murder in Roxbury slaying of woman, 61
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: A 16-year-old from Needham has been charged with murder in the October 2025 shooting death of Marie Molea-Anthony in Roxbury.
The suspect, already in custody on unrelated firearm charges, faces additional counts of murder, aggravated assault, and unlawful firearm possession.
Boston Police continue to investigate the case and are urging anyone with information to contact them or submit anonymous tips.

title: Chelsea man released decades after murder conviction won’t be retried, prosecutors say
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Thomas Rosa Jr., convicted in 1993, was released after 34 years due to new DNA evidence questioning his 1985 murder conviction.
The Suffolk County District Attorney dropped charges against Rosa citing lost evidence, new forensic testing, and law changes.
Rosa, the 100th Massachusetts exoneree since 1989, now supports others wrongfully convicted through the Exoneree Network community.

title: Former Attleboro man gets 35-40 years in prison for raping 5-year-old niece
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: William Machado, 35, received a 35-40 year prison sentence for raping his 5-year-old niece in Attleboro.
The sentence includes 10 years of supervised probation after his prison term, as ruled by Fall River Superior Court.
Machado was convicted on multiple counts, including aggravated rape of a child and indecent assault and battery.

title: A month after tragic shooting, Colin Dorgan, Blackstone Valley hockey triumph to win R.I. state title
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Colin Dorgan's family members were killed in a shooting during Blackstone Valley's 'Senior Night' game in February.
Blackstone Valley hockey team won its first Rhode Island Division II state title, defeating Lincoln High School 3-2 in quadruple overtime.
Dorgan, wearing a jersey with a heart emblem for his lost family members, forced overtime in the championship with a late goal.

title: Bruins call up former 1st-round pick Lukas Reichel on emergency basis
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Bruins called up Lukas Reichel from Providence on an emergency basis before facing the Winnipeg Jets.
Reichel, a former 2020 first-round pick, was acquired from the Vancouver Canucks on March 6 for a 2026 sixth-round pick.
In three games with Providence, Reichel recorded five points and aims to boost the Bruins' forward lineup.

title: World Cup games at Gillette no longer in jeopardy after Foxborough officially approves license
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Foxborough approved an event license, securing seven World Cup games at Gillette Stadium.
The license addresses $7.8 million in security costs, covered by Boston Soccer 2026 and Kraft Group.
The plan includes a $1.5 million escrow for safety equipment and expanded MBTA services for 21,000 patrons.
